name: New Mexico Speech-Language Pathologist License
description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to New Mexico - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Speech-Language Pathology, Audiology and Hearing Aid Dispensing Practices Board
provider.parentOrganization: New Mexico Regulation and Licensing Department

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a New Mexico SLP license cost?
Is New Mexico part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
Does New Mexico allow telepractice for SLPs?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, with a valid NM license.

mainEntity.name: Does New Mexico have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers speech-language pathologist practice in New Mexico. To work in New Mexico, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to New Mexico?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 weeks.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in New Mexico while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
